title: T-002 — Migration: bom_headers, bom_lines, bom_co_products, bom_snapshots
prompt:
# T-002 — Migration: bom_headers, bom_lines, bom_co_products, bom_snapshots

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §5.2, §7.1, §7.2
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Drizzle migration creating BOM headers/lines/co-products and immutable bom_snapshots, all RLS-scoped.

bom_snapshots.work_order_id stays UUID nullable — FK will be added in 08-PRODUCTION. snapshot_json must be NOT NULL JSONB. Statuses are TEXT with CHECK constraints (no DB enum types — keeps L3 extensibility). is_phantom and is_byproduct default false. Add index idx_bom_snapshots_wo on bom_snapshots(org_id, work_order_id) to speed lookups.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- db/migrations/0xxx_bom.sql [create]
- db/schema/bom.ts [create]
- tests/db/bom.migration.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- Item table (T-001)
- BOM Generator job code (T-019)
- BOM API endpoints (T-013..016)

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a clean DB, when migration runs, then bom_headers, bom_lines, bom_co_products, bom_snapshots exist with FK and CHECK constraints per §5.2.
2. Given a bom_header is deleted, when querying bom_lines and bom_co_products, then dependent rows are removed (ON DELETE CASCADE).
3. Given RLS enabled, when org context is missing, then no rows are visible across all four tables.
4. Given the migration is re-applied, when it runs twice, then it is idempotent.

## Test strategy
- Write RED tests asserting CASCADE delete, RLS isolation, and CHECK constraints.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/db/bom.migration.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not add work_order FK constraint here — table lives in 08-PRODUCTION.
- Do not write any seed BOM rows.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
